figures.caption: Fig. 3. Overall framework of the proposed PILOT.
Fig. 8. Validation cross-entropy by generation order. (a) Geometric orders. (b) Physics-guided orders. (c) Hybrid strategies.

Fig. 9. Predictive entropy and vertical gradient error measurements. (a) Sample-averaged predictive entropy H̄(n) over 256 steps on RadioMapSeer. (b) H̄(n) on RadioMap3DSeer under zero-shot transfer. (c) CDF of vertical gradient error on UrbanRadio3D.
Fig. 1. Overview of the PILOT framework.

Fig. 4. (a) Wavefront propagation paths in an urban scene, routing around blocked regions through lower-cost detours. (b) Visible prefix comparison for the same target patch (red): the raster-order prefix (green) consists of spatially preceding patches largely irrelevant to the target propagation state, whereas the wavefront-order prefix (yellow) concentrates along the propagation route to the target.
Fig. 7. 3D radio maps on UrbanRadio3D at receiver heights 1–4 m.
Fig. 2. Blockage ratio computation. K sample points are placed along the ground-plane projection from utx to uj ; blue segments indicate blocked portions where zk < H(xk, yk).
Fig. 10. Spatial entropy maps for three propagation regimes.
Fig. 5. Predicted radio maps. (a) 2D construction on RadioMapSeer. (b) Zero-shot transfer to RadioMap3DSeer under frequency and geometry shift.
